alternatives_rejected: sqlx (simpler but less control over multi-statement transactions)
candidates.rationale: PostgREST is Haskell; a Rust-native implementation with deadpool-postgres gives full control over the three SET LOCAL RLS statements per transaction. axum is already the gateway framework. sqlx is an alternate pool but deadpool-postgres gives finer per-connection control needed for the RLS injection pattern.

alternatives_rejected: async-graphql for Q/M (would require reimplementing Postgres schema introspection)
candidates.rationale: pg_graphql runs inside Postgres as a pgrx extension — zero network hop, full RLS context already set. The Forge approach is a thin passthrough: `SELECT graphql.resolve($query, $vars)` under the RLS context. Proven in Supabase at scale. Subscription operations use async-graphql as a separate sibling schema (see lc-003).
candidates.notes: PG18 compatibility is OQ-3 (no released build; may require build-from-source SHA). Blocks p2.

candidates.rationale: async-graphql's dynamic schema builder generates subscription types from the same introspection pg_graphql uses. graphql-transport-ws protocol support is built-in. The subscription resolvers pull from fdb-realtime's ChangeStreamSource, not from pg_graphql.

alternatives_rejected: PgBouncer (external, transaction mode breaks SET LOCAL)
candidates.rationale: SET LOCAL only survives the current transaction — not a persistent connection session. deadpool-postgres gives per-connection lifecycle hooks for the three SET LOCAL statements before every query. PgBouncer in transaction mode is incompatible with SET LOCAL semantics.

Wasmtime is the reference WASM Component Model implementation. Cranelift AOT compilation avoids JIT at function invocation. wasi:http/proxy world is Wasmtime-native. The WASM Component Model's WIT contracts give fine-grained capability control that V8 cannot match.
wasm-tools is the canonical toolchain for WASM Component Model: WIT validation, component composition, inspect. Required for c003 gate. Install: `cargo install wasm-tools`.

alternatives_rejected: NATS (LogBroker trait keeps this as swap path)
candidates.rationale: Already committed in FRF architecture. Pre-1.0 risk is mitigated by the LogBroker trait abstraction — NATS or Redpanda can be swapped by changing the Cargo feature. The GQAdonis fork tracks fixes needed for the Flint use case.

alternatives_rejected: pgrx 0.12 (PG17 only — existing ext-flint-auth uses this; keep for legacy)
candidates.rationale: pgrx 0.18.1 is the current release and supports PG18. Already used in flint_vault. flint_llm, flint_auth, flint_hooks will target PG18 with 0.18.1. Note: ext-flint-auth uses 0.12 (PG17) — do not upgrade it until PG18 auth extension is proven.

alternatives_rejected: AES-GCM (128-bit nonce, collision risk at high volume)
candidates.rationale: Already implemented in ext-flint-vault (489 lines, production-complete). 24-byte XChaCha nonce eliminates nonce-reuse risk. HKDF-SHA256 per-category subkeys enable independent rotation. DEK never selectable from SQL.

alternatives_rejected: oauth2 crate (too heavy for JWT verify only)
candidates.rationale: forge-identity already has the Claims struct and RlsContext; `verify_and_build()` is todo!(). The jsonwebtoken crate handles HS256/RS256/ES256. JWKS endpoint is fetched from flint-gate (issuer/.well-known/jwks.json). forge-identity implements this in p1-c001.

pg_cron is the standard Postgres scheduling extension. Add to PG18 Dockerfile alongside existing extensions. Supabase ships it; Flint should too. One apt-get or build-from-source addition to images/postgres18/Dockerfile.

candidates.chosen: S3-compatible backend (MinIO self-hosted or Cloudflare R2) + Postgres metadata table + Vault credentials + Keto access
alternatives_rejected: Build object storage primitives from scratch (unnecessary)
candidates.rationale: Don't build object storage primitives. Build the Forge-specific layer: Postgres metadata table (file path, owner, ACL, mime type, size, checksum), Vault-encrypted presigned URL signing credentials, Keto permission check at file access. The fke-store-s3 crate (Kiln component store, already designed) can be the S3 client foundation. Phase 6.

candidates.chosen: cedar-policy crate via existing UAR integration
alternatives_rejected: Casbin (not as expressive for capability contexts)
candidates.rationale: Cedar is already the chosen policy engine in PAUX-1/UAR. Flint Forge wires Cedar for Kiln capability gating and Quarry mutation authZ. Don't duplicate the Cedar engine; wire the existing UAR Cedar service.

alternatives_rejected: SpiceDB (SpiceDB is an alternative to Keto; Keto already chosen)
candidates.rationale: Already chosen in FRF architecture. Keto's check API provides subscribe-time topic visibility gate and per-event fan-out gate. The gRPC client is standard; check-cache with tuple-delete invalidation is the required scaling mitigation (OQ-6).

candidates.chosen: liter-llm (Rust LLM routing library) + pgrx BGW
alternatives_rejected: Direct provider SDKs in pgrx (violates sovereign routing model)
plv8 + JS LLM (V8 in Postgres, not Rust-native)
candidates.rationale: liter-llm provides the routing/provider-abstraction layer. The BGW (background worker) hosts the tokio runtime + liter-llm without blocking Postgres backends. Surface 1 (sync) requires careful interrupt handling (CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS / WaitLatch). Surface 2 (async queue) is the safe default. Phase 4.
